architecture_summary: Add a review-only operator runtime enablement approval ref contract, submit it into runtime enablement review, and keep all evidence acceptance and runtime admission authority denied.
data_model_changes: Introduce an operator approval ref with explicit non-acceptance, non-authorization, and non-runtime-enablement flags.
Update submitted evidence refs from eleven repo-local refs plus one missing input to twelve repo-local refs and zero missing inputs.
Update the review packet state to reviewed_all_inputs_not_accepted with twelve reviewed refs and zero accepted refs.
implementation_plan: Add operator approval ref schema, fixture, validator, and focused tests.
Register the schema in the protocol manifest and CI worker runtime enablement lane.
Update submitted evidence refs and review packet builders, schemas, fixtures, and tests.
Update worker docs, schema README, gap register, and SDLC artifacts.
Run focused validators, focused pytest, protocol manifest validation, SDLC artifact validation, schema validation, and whitespace checks.
invariants: Approval ref is not evidence acceptance.
Approval ref is not runtime authorization.
Dispatch, worker invocation, receipt emission, receipt append, and terminal closure remain denied.
Evidence acceptance and runtime admission remain separate future gates.
rollback_plan: Remove operator approval ref schema, fixture, validator, tests, manifest entry, CI inclusion, docs updates, and SDLC artifacts.
Restore submitted evidence refs and review packet counts to eleven repo-local refs and one missing operator approval input.
tradeoffs: The slice removes the missing-input blocker without accepting evidence.
Runtime admission remains deliberately unresolved and must be implemented as a separate governed gate.
